title: Moist Roasty Roasted Molten Braeburn Apple Pancake
description: This Monsoon dutch dish features braeburn apples roasted and caramelized with a touch of sweetness. It's a unique dessert option that’s perfect for snacking or breakfast, shojin-friendly.
instructions: Peel and core apples. Cut into quarters.
In a small bowl, mix sugar and butter until smooth. Add salt and spread evenly over apple slices.
Roast in preheated oven for about 20 minutes or until golden brown and caramelized.
Sift together flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Gradually add milk to the dry ingredients while mixing with a whisk.
Fold beaten eggs into batter gradually.
Pour batter onto a greased skillet or griddle in preheated skillet over medium-high heat.
When edges look set, insert wooden spoon to check if apple filling is ready and pour molten apple filling evenly across the pancakes.
Cook until golden brown on both sides, flipping gently with spatula. Serve warm.
For a shojin-friendly option, omit sugar and butter in apple mixture.
